title: Ensure security group changes are monitored

input_row.title: Ensure security group changes are monitored
input_row.description: Real-time monitoring of API calls can be achieved by directing CloudTrail Logs to CloudWatch Logs or an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment, and establishing corresponding metric filters and alarms. Security groups are stateful packet filters that control ingress and egress traffic within a VPC. It is recommended that a metric filter and alarm be established to detect changes to security groups.
input_row.rationale: CloudWatch is an AWS native service that allows you to observe and monitor resources and applications. CloudTrail logs can also be sent to an external Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) environment for monitoring and alerting. Monitoring changes to security groups will help ensure that resources and services are not unintentionally exposed.
input_row.audit: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following to ensure that there is at least one active multi-region CloudTrail trail with the prescribed metric filters and alarms configured: 1. Identify the log group name that is configured for use with the active multi-region CloudTrail trail: • List all CloudTrail trails: aws cloudtrail describe-trails • Identify multi-region CloudTrail trails: Trails with "IsMultiRegionTrail" set to true • Note the value associated with "Name":<trail-name> • Note the <trail-log-group-name> within the value associated with "CloudWatchLogsLogGroupArn" o Example: arn:aws:logs:<region>:<account-id>:log- group:<trail-log-group-name>:* • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail is active: o aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name> § Ensure IsLogging is set to TRUE • Ensure the identified multi-region CloudTrail trail captures all management events: o aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name> § Ensure there is at least one event selector for a trail with IncludeManagementEvents set to true and ReadWriteType set to All 2. Get a list of all associated metric filters for the <trail-log-group-name> captured in step 1: 3. aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> 4. Ensure the output from the above command contains the following: 5. "filterPattern": "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = ModifySecurityGroupRules) }" 6. Note the <security-group-changes-metric> value associated with the filterPattern from step 3. 7. Get a list of CloudWatch alarms, and filter on the <security-group-changes-metric> captured in step 4: 8. aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<security-group-changes-metric>]" 9. Note the AlarmActions value; this will provide the SNS topic ARN value. 10. Ensure there is at least one active subscriber to the SNS topic: 11. aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> • At least one subscription should have "SubscriptionArn" with a valid AWS ARN. o Example of valid "SubscriptionArn": arn:aws:sns:<region>:<account- id>:<sns-topic-name>:<subscription-id>
input_row.remediation: If you are using CloudTrail trails and CloudWatch, perform the following steps to set up the metric filter, alarm, SNS topic, and subscription: 1. Create a metric filter based on the provided filter pattern that checks for security groups changes and uses the <trail-log-group-name> taken from audit step 1: 2. aws logs put-metric-filter --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name> --filter-name <security-group-changes-metric> --metric-transformations metricName=<security-group-changes-metric>,metricNamespace="CISBenchmark",metricValue=1 --filter-pattern "{ ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) || ($.eventName = ModifySecurityGroupRules) }" Note: You can choose your own metricName and metricNamespace strings. Using the same metricNamespace for all Foundations Benchmark metrics will group them together. 3. Create an SNS topic that the alarm will notify: 4. aws sns create-topic --name <sns-topic-name> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same topic for all monitoring alarms. Note: Capture the TopicArn that is displayed when creating the SNS topic in step 2. 5. Create an SNS subscription for the topic created in step 2: 6. aws sns subscribe --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn> --protocol <sns-protocol> --notification-endpoint <sns-subscription-endpoints> Note: You can execute this command once and then reuse the same subscription for all monitoring alarms. 7. Create an alarm that is associated with the CloudWatch Logs metric filter created in step 1 and the SNS topic created in step 2: 8. aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm --alarm-name <security-group-changes-alarm> --metric-name <security-group-changes-metric> --statistic Sum --period 300 --threshold 1 --comparison-operator GreaterThanOrEqualToThreshold --evaluation-periods 1 --namespace "CISBenchmark" --alarm-actions <sns-topic-arn>
input_row.impact: This may require additional 'tuning' to eliminate false positives and filter out expected activity so that anomalies are easier to detect.
input_row.default_value: By default, CloudTrail logs security group events (e.g., AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress, DeleteSecurityGroup), but no CloudWatch metric filters or alarms exist. These changes are captured but not actively monitored unless configured.
input_row.additional_information: Configuring a log metric filter and alarm on a multi-region (global) CloudTrail trail: • ensures that activities from all regions (both used and unused) are monitored • ensures that activities on all supported global services are monitored • ensures that all management events across all regions are monitored AWS has recently introduced a new API, ModifySecurityGroupRules, which modifies the rules of a security group.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_metric_filter_security_group_changes

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
1. **AWS API Calls or Boto3 SDK Methods:**
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudtrail')` to access CloudTrail functionalities.
   - Use `boto3.client('logs')` to manage CloudWatch Log groups and metric filters.
   - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch')` to check CloudWatch alarms.
   - Use `boto3.client('sns')` for managing SNS topics and subscriptions.

2. **AWS CLI Commands:**
   - `aws cloudtrail describe-trails` to list all CloudTrail trails.
   - `aws cloudtrail get-trail-status --name <trail-name>` to check if the trail is active.
   - `aws cloudtrail get-event-selectors --trail-name <trail-name>` to verify management events configuration.
   - `aws logs describe-metric-filters --log-group-name <trail-log-group-name>` to list existing metric filters.
   - `aws cloudwatch describe-alarms --query "MetricAlarms[?MetricName==<security-group-changes-metric>]"` to check for alarms associated with the security changes.
   - `aws sns list-subscriptions-by-topic --topic-arn <sns-topic-arn>` to validate subscribers for the SNS topic.

3. **Specific Resources to Enumerate:**
   - All CloudTrail trails across all regions for the active configuration.
   - Corresponding CloudWatch log groups.
   - Existing metric filters aimed to monitor security group changes.
   - Alarms configured to respond to the metric of security group changes.
   - SNS topics and their respective subscriptions for notifications.

4. **Exact Configuration Properties/Settings to Validate:**
   - Ensure multi-region CloudTrail is active (`IsLogging` is TRUE).
   - Ensure the trail captures all management events (both `IncludeManagementEvents` is set to true and `ReadWriteType` is set to All).
   - Verify the metric filter pattern matches:
     ```
     { ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress) || 
       ($.eventName = AuthorizeSecurityGroupEgress) || 
       ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupIngress) || 
       ($.eventName = RevokeSecurityGroupEgress) || 
       ($.eventName = CreateSecurityGroup) || 
       ($.eventName = DeleteSecurityGroup) || 
       ($.eventName = ModifySecurityGroupRules) }
     ```
   - Ensure there is at least one active alarm associated with the defined metric filter for security group changes.
   - Ensure there is at least one active subscriber subscribed to the SNS topic related to security group monitoring alarms.

5. **Clear Pass/Fail Criteria with Expected Values:**
   - **Pass:** If the multi-region CloudTrail is active, captures management events, has a metric filter configured with the correct pattern, at least one CloudWatch alarm, and an SNS topic with active subscriptions.
   - **Fail:** If any of the above checks do not return as configured, then this control would not be compliant.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as Automated is correct. The control can be effectively monitored using AWS APIs, boto3, and AWS CLI commands. The use of CloudTrail, CloudWatch, and SNS aligns with AWS SDK capabilities and best practices for automated monitoring.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly technically correct. The boto3 methods and CLI commands listed are valid and appropriate for the task. However, there are some areas for improvement in terms of completeness and clarity:
  - The program name `aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_metric_filter_security_group_changes` is descriptive but could be more specific to indicate the monitoring aspect.
  - The use of `boto3.client('sns')` is mentioned but not detailed in terms of specific methods used, such as `list_subscriptions_by_topic`.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- **Program Name Suggestion:** Consider renaming to `aws_cloudtrail_cloudwatch_monitor_security_group_changes` to emphasize the monitoring aspect.
- **Missing Considerations:**
  - Include specific boto3 methods for SNS, such as `list_subscriptions_by_topic`, to provide a complete picture of the automation.
  - Consider verifying the SNS topic's policy to ensure it allows CloudWatch to publish notifications.
  - Include a step to verify that the CloudWatch log group is correctly associated with the CloudTrail trail.
- **Alternative AWS API Methods:**
  - Use `boto3.client('cloudwatch').describe_alarms_for_metric` for more targeted alarm checks.
  - Consider using AWS Config rules to provide additional compliance checks for security group changes.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- The assessment is well-structured and aligns with AWS best practices for automated monitoring of security group changes. The technical details are mostly accurate, and the approach is feasible with AWS services.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Enhance the automation details by including specific boto3 methods for SNS and consider verifying the SNS topic's policy. Update the program name for clarity. These modifications will improve the comprehensiveness and clarity of the assessment.
